page: Amazon Web Services r8gd.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server powered by custom AWS Graviton processors. Built on the ARMv9 architecture using Neoverse-V2 cores, this instance provides 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz, with no hyperthreading. It features 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding an 8.0 GB per core ratio. Local storage is provided via a bundled 2850 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. Network connectivity is supported by a 22 Gbps baseline bandwidth managed through the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier memory performance, with uncached large-block read bandwidth reaching 402.07 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 201.0 GB/sec. Multi-core compute capabilities are strong, as demonstrated by a Geekbench multi-core score of 23486.0 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 503.69 GB/sec. In database-specific testing, the server achieved a top-tier PassMark database operations score of 47307.31 and sustained 100.54 million operations per second in Redis SET operations. Single-core performance remains in the average tier, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1854.0.

The ARM-based architecture and high memory-to-vCPU density offer qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound applications compared to traditional x86 alternatives. The integration of high-speed local NVMe storage reduces the need for external block storage for temporary data. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale database operations, software compilation, and high-throughput static web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gd.12xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized server powered by 48 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz. It features 384.0 GB of RAM, a bundled 2850 GB NVMe SSD local instance store, and 22 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results highlight top-tier memory performance, with uncached large-block read speeds of 402.07 GB/sec and write speeds of 201.0 GB/sec. Multi-core performance is strong, evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 23486.0 and a PassMark database operations score of 47307.31. Single-core performance is moderate, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1854.0. This server is optimized for memory-intensive databases, high-throughput web serving, and software compilation tasks requiring substantial local storage and high memory bandwidth.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8gd.12xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized server featuring 48 Graviton cores, 384 GB RAM, and a 2850 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8gd.12xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized server with 48 Graviton cores, 384 GB RAM, and 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An ARM-based memory-optimized server with dedicated physical cores, high-speed local NVMe storage, and top-tier memory bandwidth for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores at 2.8 GHz
384.0 GB of system memory with top-tier read and write bandwidth
Bundled 2850 GB local NVMe SSD instance store volume
22 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed via Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier performance in database operations and multi-threaded decompression
Suited for memory-intensive databases, software compilation, and web serving
